nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Welles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Wells
meanings: English: habitational name from Wells next the Sea (Norfolk) or Wells (Somerset), both named with the plural of Old English wella ‘spring, stream’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a group of springs or streams.
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Dupuis   ‘from the well’.
History: One of numerous early immigrants from England bearing this name was Thomas Welles, governor of colonial CT, who was in Hartford, CT, by 1636.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Welles was born about 1115, in Well, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Walter Fitz Ragemer DeWelles, was 29 and his mother, Amaranth Delincoln, was 33. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Maud de Gaunt. He died in 1198, in Spilsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Welles est né environ 1115, à Well, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Walter Fitz Ragemer DeWelles, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Amaranth Delincoln, avait 33 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Maud de Gaunt. Il est décédé en 1198, à Spilsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.Wells, Norfolk, England: Wells next the Sea, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom

Er hatte mit Maud de Gaunt mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1198 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Spilsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Wells, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 웰스 니스트 테 시
ko.lifeSummary: William de Welles(이)가 대략 1115년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter Fitz Ragemer DeWelles(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Amaranth Delincoln(은)는 33세였다. 그는 Maud de Gaunt(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1198년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스피스비에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.
